questions.question: What color t-shirt did Jesse Pollak say he would be wearing at Frontier Tower?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak mentioned he would be at Frontier Tower wearing an orange t-shirt and a whale face.

questions.question: Which cricket team's colors did Jesse Pollak's kids learn are black?
questions.explanation: In his post about the cricket World Cup, Jesse mentioned his kids now know New Zealand's colors are black.

questions.question: Which game did Jesse Pollak mention pulling all-nighters for during grad school at USC?
questions.explanation: Jesse recalled playing DoTA or LoL at USC's GamePipe Lab during grad school.

questions.question: How much did Brian Armstrong charge for an 'Ask Me Anything' session via a beep?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong thanked @bigeon for a beep and offered an AMA for $0.25.

questions.question: What is the name of the Gnomie Jesse Pollak claimed in the Sanctuary?
questions.explanation: Jesse claimed 'Scruffy Duskhollow' in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary.

questions.question: In March 2024, what did Base Official report about their revenue?
questions.explanation: Base Official posted on 2024-03-15 that they doubled their revenue the previous week.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak mention as his 'face' for the Frontier Tower meetup?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned he would be the one with the 'whale face' at the meetup.

questions.question: Which service did Brian Armstrong use a referral for to get one month free?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong thanked @ace for a referral to noscroll.com.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak requested feedback on a FIP for which specific project?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak was looking for feedback on the 'Snapchain Signers FIP'.

questions.question: Which mainstream company did Dan Romero express excitement about working with in April 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned being excited about working with DoorDash as a tangible mainstream use case for crypto.

questions.question: What are Jesse Pollak's kids now asking for their own farms in?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted that his kids are asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms.

questions.question: What classic game did Brian Armstrong ask if a Farcaster snap could run?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong shared a link asking 'But can it run DOOM?' regarding a Farcaster snap.

questions.question: Which Coinbase/Farcaster ecosystem member shared they are on the autism spectrum?
questions.explanation: Mircea Pasoi (coinbase handle) shared a blog post about discovering they are on the autism spectrum.

questions.question: Where can developers go to find daily reward credits to build, according to Jesse Pollak?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned daily reward credits to build on neynar.com/studio.

questions.question: Who did Brian Armstrong thank for a 'beep'?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong's post said: 'thanks for the beep @bigeon'.

questions.question: According to Dan Romero, what is a common myth about the MPP (Machine Payments Protocol)?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero listed 'MPP only works with Tempo' as a myth, stating it is payment method agnostic.

questions.question: At what approximate block height did the snapchain mainnet shard 1 halt in April 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak reported that snapchain mainnet shard 1 halted at ~32M height.

questions.question: What specific edge case was Jesse Pollak working on regarding wallet balance inaccuracies?
questions.explanation: Jesse noted an edge case where tokens with 'super low liquidity USDC pairs' were not priced correctly.

questions.question: Which shard was expected to see instability during a validator set cutover on Feb 24?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak expected instability when shard 0 cut over to the updated validator set.

questions.question: What tool did Brian Armstrong decide to stop using in favor of 'vanilla Claude code'?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong mentioned going back to vanilla Claude code after using GSD-2.

questions.question: How many payment methods did the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) launch with on Day 1?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero stated MPP launched with 4 payment methods: Tempo, Stripe, Visa, and Lightspark.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak compared the future of LLM-powered roleplay to which fictional object?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated 'Tom Riddle’s diary will no longer be fiction' due to LLMs/agents roleplaying with knowledge bases.

questions.question: Who is Jesse Pollak collaborating with to build the Farcaster protocol itself?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned that he and @rish from the Neynar team are moving from infrastructure to building the protocol itself.

questions.question: What was the result of mcilroyc's latency improvement after Jesse's team implemented changes?
questions.explanation: Jesse shared a graph showing '50% less global p95 latency' for mcilroyc.

questions.question: Brian Armstrong said he would only say 'wowow' when what feature returns?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted: 'i'm only gonna say wowow when animated pfps come back'.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak noted that 'skill issue' has a whole new meaning now. What was the context of this timeframe?
questions.explanation: Given the proximity to posts about agents and coding, Jesse remarked on the changing meaning of 'skill issue'.

questions.question: What time window was specified for Farcaster's backend database layer changes on Feb 5, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse announced DB changes between noon and 1PM PST on Feb 5th.

questions.question: Who does Jesse Pollak credit with a mission to 'send bugs right to jail'?
questions.explanation: Jesse mentions that @grin is on a mission to send bugs right to jail, describing the 'Neynar way'.

questions.question: What API did Brian Armstrong ask Neynar for on March 25, 2026?
options: To check if a user is a builder
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong asked @neynar if they had an API to check if a user is a builder (miniapp dev, project owner, etc).

questions.question: Where did Jesse Pollak stay up late building software to power Farcaster?
questions.explanation: Jesse referenced staying up late this week similar to his days at USC's GamePipe Lab.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak say about AI in relation to cities like Detroit or San Jose?
options: You don't get a Detroit or San Jose with AI
AI needs a physical hub like San Jose
questions.explanation: Jesse quoted: 'when it comes to AI, you don't get a Detroit or a San Jose'.

questions.question: What game metaphor did Dan Romero use to describe his 'new personal ATH'?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero used terms like 'Brainstorm copy', 'flush', 'steel cards', and 'bloodstone', referencing the game Balatro.

questions.question: To which organization has the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) been submitted as a web standard?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned that MPP has been submitted to the IETF as a web standard.

questions.question: Which social network did Dan Romero mention as having 800M WAU but still finding retention hard?
questions.explanation: While not explicitly named in the snippet, Dan mentions '800M WAU' social networks, which in March 2024 context usually refers to Telegram.

questions.question: When did Jesse Pollak put 'Studio on web' for Neynar?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted on Jan 23, 2026, about putting Studio on the web at neynar.com/studio.

questions.question: Who did India defeat in the cricket World Cup final Jesse watched?
options: Not explicitly stated (implied NZ or others)
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned India won and his kids learned New Zealand's colors, but the opponent isn't explicitly named as the finalist in the text.

questions.question: What project was Brian Armstrong's 'share of the day' on March 14, 2026?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong shared a video/repo for a project called CorridorKey.

questions.question: What time was the planned rollout for authentication and user management upgrades on dev.neynar.com?
questions.explanation: Jesse planned the rollout for tomorrow (from Jan 13) between 8-8:30am PST.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak claim in the 'Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary'?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on 2026-04-21 explicitly states: 'I claimed Scruffy Duskhollow in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary!'.

questions.question: Which payment rail extensions were mentioned as being built for MPP in a 'matter of days'?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned that 'Visa and Lightspark were each able to extend MPP in a matter of days'.

questions.question: According to Dan Romero, what is the 'MPP myth' regarding session-based vs. pay-per-request?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero stated 'MPP supports both' in response to the myth that it is one or the other.

questions.question: What specific blockchain does Dan Romero mention has a 'draft extension' for MPP?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero noted there's a 'draft extension for Solana' for MPP.

questions.question: What was the specific pricing issue Jesse Pollak identified with wallet balances?
options: NFTs were priced in ETH not USD
questions.explanation: Jesse identified that 'tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs' were not priced correctly.

questions.question: Why does Dan Romero claim MPP is 'faster in practice'?
questions.explanation: Dan stated that 'Fewer API round trips also means MPP is faster in practice' regarding session-based payments.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak mentioned giving an agent access to a 'humanoid' called what?
questions.explanation: Jesse asked if someone had given an OpenClaw agent access to a humanoid like the 'G1'.

questions.question: Why did Brian Armstrong say he couldn't attend a specific event spirit notwithstanding?
options: His waifu needed help changing diapers
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted: 'i wish i could go, my waifu needs help changing our little one's diapers'.

questions.question: What specific Neynar feature does Jesse Pollak say helps 'improve the bot faster'?
questions.explanation: Jesse explicitly stated: 'tool call visibility helps us improve the bot faster'.

questions.question: What was the date of Base Official's first cast?
questions.explanation: Base Official's post on 2022-10-24 says 'First cast - can’t wait to see where this road takes me!'.

questions.question: What did Coinbase ecosystem member Mircea Pasoi build to learn about Hubs?
questions.explanation: Mircea Pasoi built an (incomplete) Python port of @farcaster/hub-nodejs to learn about Hubs.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak mentioned moving a Farcaster mainnet validator to another continent to pave the way for what?
options: Onboarding someone else to run a validator from a different geo
questions.explanation: Jesse stated moving validators helps 'pave the way to onboard someone else to run a validator from a different geo'.
